typical_monthly_usd: $345/mo Marketing add-on on top of Tekmetric SMS base ($179-$439/mo). Total stack: ~$524-$840/mo for single-location shop. Legacy Shopgenie pricing was Lite $345 / Pro $495.
setup_fee_usd: $0 — Tekmetric advertises no setup or hidden fees [uncertain: whether legacy Shopgenie onboarding fee carried over for migrated accounts]
min_ad_spend_usd: None — Tekmetric Marketing is CRM/lifecycle, not paid media management. Shop runs its own Google Ads or hires separate agency.
contract_length_months: Month-to-month. Tekmetric explicitly markets 'no contracts'. Annual prepay available for ~10% discount on base SMS.
cancellation_policy: No long-term contract; cancel any time per Tekmetric public messaging. [uncertain: specific notice period in MSA]
asset_ownership: Shop owns customer data, domain, and website content. Website built on Tekmetric/Shopgenie platform — likely platform-hosted, so moving off may require rebuild. [uncertain: full data export terms post-cancellation]
pricing_transparency: Published — base SMS tiers and Marketing add-on price ($345/mo) listed on tekmetric.com/pricing and confirmed by Capterra/G2/GetApp. Legacy Shopgenie Lite/Pro tiers ($345/$495) on softwarefinder.com.
known_pain_points: Can't always use existing main phone number for call tracking (per G2 reviews)
Acquisition integration still rolling out in phases — feature parity between legacy Shopgenie and new Tekmetric Marketing is in flux through 2025-2026
Tekmetric SMS is required to fully use Marketing — locks shop into the broader platform
Website is built on platform — limited portability if shop leaves
notable_clients: [uncertain: specific named shop logos not published]
pros: Tight native integration with Tekmetric SMS — no Zapier or middleware
No long-term contract, transparent published pricing
Single-vendor stack reduces data silos (SMS + CRM + marketing in one)
Strong CRM automation: declined-services reactivation, reviews, two-way text
Free online booking tier as lead-gen entry point
Built by/for auto repair — not a generic CRM retrofitted
cons: No paid-media management — shop still needs an ads agency for Google/Meta
Only relevant if shop uses or migrates to Tekmetric SMS
Doesn't serve collision/body shops well (workflow built for mechanical RO lifecycle, not CCC/DRP)
Mid-acquisition integration risk — Shopgenie legacy customers being migrated to new product
koffman_relevance: Direct overlap with Koffman's current Turnkey-Shopgenie stack on the AR side. Koffman currently pays Turnkey as the reseller/agency layer on top of Shopgenie. Going direct to Tekmetric Marketing would eliminate Turnkey's markup but lose the done-for-you content/social/gift-bag services Turnkey provides. NOT a fit for Koffman's collision department — Tekmetric Marketing is mechanical-only and has no DRP/CCC/insurance workflow. If Koffman keeps Tekmetric SMS on the AR side, switching to native Tekmetric Marketing is the obvious cost play vs. paying Turnkey to resell Shopgenie.
uncertain: setup_fee_usd (legacy Shopgenie onboarding terms for migrated accounts)
